title: Kickxia commutata
taxonomy: Kickxia commutata is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Kickxia and the order Lamiales. The classification further breaks down to the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, and the clade Asterids. Kickxia commutata is also known to be a member of the tribe Veroniceae. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.

intro: Kickxia commutata, also known as Common Field Chickweed, is a perennial plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that grows as a weed in many parts of the world. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease. The bioactive constituents of Kickxia commutata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Common herbal preparations made with Kickxia commutata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin conditions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
